headline: Youth Political Participation Shifts to Online Petitions Over Parties
context: A 2025 Merdeka Center survey found 68% of youth prefer online activism over joining political parties.

cards.big: 68% of Malaysian youth aged 18-30 prefer online petitions to party membership
cards.sub: Only 3.2% of registered party members nationwide are under 25

cards.big: Change.org Malaysia recorded 4,200 youth-initiated petitions in 2025 alone
cards.sub: Environmental issues education reform and cost of living dominate topics

cards.big: Malaysian political parties have an average member age of 52 as of 2025 data
cards.sub: Youth wings exist in all major parties but hold minimal decision-making power

cards.big: Taiwan's digital democracy platform vTaiwan engages 200,000 citizens in policy
cards.sub: Structured online participation can complement traditional party politics

cards.big: Youth are not apathetic about politics—they are apathetic about political parties
Should political parties be required to reserve leadership positions for members under 35
